authoritative_sources.title: Medicaid Non-Emergency Medical Transportation (NEMT) and related support
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public program guidance for how Medicaid and Medicare Advantage plans specify transportation assistance, beneficiary responsibilities, and member access expectations.
used_for: handoff context expectations for transportation services

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 440 - State Medicaid plans
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regulatory baseline for federal Medicaid plan operations, including service coverage framework and recordkeeping obligations that bound coordination of supportive services.
used_for: defining what public authority requirements are likely to govern transport support
identifying where state-level interpretation can materially change local handling

authoritative_sources.title: CMS State Plan Evidence and Managed-Care Instruction set
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative CMS managed-care instruction baseline used when coordination work depends on how plans document transport obligations, authorizations, and reporting requirements.
used_for: documentation standards for records moved across teams
policy review points when source interpretations conflict

authoritative_sources.title: State Medicaid manuals, transport, and utilization instructions
authoritative_sources.publisher: State Medicaid agencies and managed-care plans
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: States implement transport operations through their own plans and policies; these references provide the operational gate conditions and routing ownership patterns used after federal baseline alignment.
used_for: owner assignment and escalation rules for blocked transport coordination

authoritative_sources.title: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HIPAA governs what protected health and disability-related information may be shared across care, dispatch, and operations teams.
used_for: artifact redaction and minimum-access practices for transport coordination

workflow_stages.name: transport handoff intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Establish case intent, elder mobility context, and required evidence before any ownership transfer or routing instruction.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: dependency mapping and systems-of-record confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Validate which records, systems, and owners control current trip status, scheduling, consent, referral, and beneficiary constraints.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: cross-team routing and blocker escalation
workflow_stages.description: Issue routing commands only when ownership, artifacts, and acceptance state are unambiguous; escalate ownership conflicts, duplicated claims, or missing evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: closure, verification, and traceability handoff
workflow_stages.description: Produce explicit handoff and closure records that prove prior-owner context, artifact continuity, and next-owner responsibilities.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: transportation request packet (requestor notes, trip goal, urgency flags, scheduling constraints)
medical referral, consent, and care-plan dependency records
beneficiary authorization and payer/plan eligibility artifacts
family or caregiver handoff notes, mobility assistance flags, and special-needs requirements
dispatch or provider readiness check records
route and assignment status from transport systems
systems_of_record: transportation dispatch or mobility management systems
member/beneficiary files containing consent and authorization
ticketing, scheduling, or referral systems for handoff ownership
audit/compliance logs for evidence transfer and blockers

allowed: validate prerequisites for coordination handoff and route ownership
produce dependency, blocker, routing, and closure artifacts
identify missing evidence and required tenant context before progressing
escalate to adjacent specialists when non-coordination authority is required
escalate: clinical-transport risk decisions, safety-clearance determinations, or rider suitability judgments
legal authority, policy override, or final payer authorization requests
route execution and provider assignment actions that require operational dispatch authority
conflicts between tenant policy and public baseline that change ownership or closure conditions
refuse: attempts to absorb execution tasks such as booking, dispatching, approving route changes, or completing trips
requests for legal, licensed, or final financial/contractual decisions
handoffs lacking prior-owner context, missing mandatory artifacts, or unresolved ownership

domain_failure_modes: handoff packets missing consent, referral, authorization, or trip status evidence
multiple teams each claiming same transport step without a defined precedence rule
owner attempts to route the specialist into dispatch, clinical transport authorization, or legal compliance decisions
jurisdiction-specific transport policy changes not reflected in request context causing unsafe closure
family/caregiver communication constraints omitted from evidence chain

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State plans and managed-care products differ in referral sources, trip-completion definitions, and escalation points; tenant policy retrieval is required before final routing posture assertions.
assumptions_recorded: Treat all in-scope transport tasks as coordination only unless tenant policy says otherwise.
Escalate immediately when jurisdictional policy materially diverges from public baselines.

notes: Research intentionally distinguishes elder-care and disability transport coordination from execution, dispatch, clinical, and legal authority.
